# Handoff — Feedback ring: structured mining + backtraceable synthesis

## Overview
Resume the feedback-ring activation track from [./](./). The prior session hardened the feedback model after review found the transcript miner was a false-positive factory that had spam-created 522 auto-tickets. The naive free-text miner is now removed and replaced with a structured-metadata-only detector; spec verified-state recomputation is now a typed outcome; the stop-hook's auto-ticket synthesis is intentionally paused; 522 spam tickets were bulk-deleted; and the remaining ring-activation work is decomposed into five dependency-linked tickets. Your job is to execute those five tickets in dependency order to re-activate the ring on a robust, backtraceable, verifiable basis using structured signals only.

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend
- `<workspace>` = the ticket/test workspace path `c:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api` (pass as `workspace` to ticket-mcp / test-mcp; ticket work lives here).
- `ACT` = activation ticket (see Ticket Legend).
- `TRACKER` = broader ring tracker (see Ticket Legend).
- `PROV`, `INGEST`, `MAP`, `BFS`, `SYNTH` = the five child tickets (see Ticket Legend).

## Ticket Legend (shorthand → full UUID + canonical title)
- `ACT` = `6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc` — [6b0002bf [feedback-api][activation] Wire feedback discovery, collection, and analyzer loop (ring activation)](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc/ticket.toml)
- `TRACKER` = `5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d` — [5d4078fa [content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring — execution→verified, transcript mining, missing-rule tickets, ticket-entity feedback](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/ticket.toml)
- `PROV` = `a7601cb7-6c92-4891-aa55-07ab46125bb8` — [a7601cb7 [feedback-api][provenance] Extend FeedbackProvenance with session/turn/tool-call backtrace refs](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a7601cb7-6c92-4891-aa55-07ab46125bb8/ticket.toml)
- `INGEST` = `b4954d6c-0451-49ed-8939-11f6568558f5` — [b4954d6c [feedback-api][ingestion] Mine explicit feedback-ingestion tool calls from structured session metadata](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b4954d6c-0451-49ed-8939-11f6568558f5/ticket.toml)
- `MAP` = `16e112a7-5ab0-45a5-87c8-7d89d07ffd16` — [16e112a7 [feedback-api][signals] Define failed-tool-call to entity mapping and recording policy](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/16e112a7-5ab0-45a5-87c8-7d89d07ffd16/ticket.toml)
- `BFS` = `3fa60398-3154-46ee-aca5-8d87541bac1e` — [3fa60398 [feedback-api][signals] Deterministic BFS entity queue for structured feedback mining](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/3fa60398-3154-46ee-aca5-8d87541bac1e/ticket.toml)
- `SYNTH` = `3d4c4739-3138-40be-947d-556e5f7de53a` — [3d4c4739 [feedback-api][analyzer] Backtraceable, verifiable follow-up ticket format; re-enable synthesis behind it](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/3d4c4739-3138-40be-947d-556e5f7de53a/ticket.toml)

## Long-Horizon Goal (epic)
Make the feedback ring genuinely self-driving from **structured signals only** (no message-text heuristics until embedding-based context reasoning lands), where every mined entry and every synthesized ticket is backtraceable to its origin turn/tool-call and is independently verifiable. Epic ticket: `ACT` (`6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc`), which now `depends_on` all five child tickets and cannot close until the ring fires end-to-end with recorded evidence.

## Findings And Decisions
- The prior miner detected "rule confusion" from free-text (tokenizer + confusion-marker prefixes + `overlap < 2`) and auto-created tickets. Run against committed `.session` transcripts (full of "error/fail/conflict") it produced 522 false-positive tickets. Decision: mine **structured metadata only**; defer content reasoning to a future embedding model.
- `SessionTurnEventMeta` already carries the right structured signals (`tool_success`, `tool_call_id`, `tool_arguments_json`) in [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs) — the detector needs no NLP.
- Decision: entity iteration must be deterministic breadth-first, appending newly-discovered entities to a queue after each unit (or, acceptable alternative, mine only entities detected at the beginning).
- Decision: only ship logic that confidently works with actionable error states encoded in the type system (drove the typed `SpecVerificationOutcome` and the "no guessing the ingestion tool-name schema" constraint).
- Decision: `.session` transcript files stay tracked in git (an interim `.gitignore` exclusion was reverted).
- Decision: the 522 mined tickets were bulk-deleted (not salvaged); investigate trigger patterns from transcripts under `MAP`.

## What Already Landed (validated — do not redo)
- Structured detector `mine_structured_feedback_signals` in [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs): detects failed tool calls via `event_meta.tool_success == Some(false)`, returns backtraceable `StructuredFeedbackSignal`; no `SessionTurn.content` inspection. Tests: 2 new, pass.
- Stop-hook [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs) now logs a structured signal summary; auto-ticket synthesis removed/paused.
- Typed `SpecVerificationOutcome` (`NoGuards` / `Pending{missing_guards}` / `Failed{failed_guards}` / `Verified`) in [./memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/verification.rs](./memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/verification.rs); `test-cli` emits structured `spec_verification` reports instead of a swallowed `Vec<String>` (see [./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/handlers.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/handlers.rs)).
- Repaired 5 pre-existing `test-cli` fixtures broken by the committed interoperability provenance contract (added `--domain/--operation/--run-id` + links) in [./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/lib_tests.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/lib_tests.rs).
- Validation: session-api 13/13, spec-api verification 3/3, test-cli 8/8. Evidence executions recorded (see Key Entities).

## Blockers
- None blocking start. `PROV` is immediately actionable (no prerequisites). Note the ordering constraint: `INGEST` needs `PROV`; `BFS` needs `INGEST`+`MAP`; `SYNTH` needs `INGEST`+`MAP`+`PROV`.

## Next Actions In Order
1. `PROV` (`a7601cb7-6c92-4891-aa55-07ab46125bb8`) — extend `FeedbackProvenance` with session/turn/tool-call refs. First concrete action: add `turn_sequence` + `tool_call_id` to `FeedbackProvenance` in feedback-api and thread them from a captured `SessionTurn`. First validation check: serde round-trip test + a mined/ingested entry asserting populated `session_id` + `turn_sequence` + `tool_call_id`.
2. `INGEST` (`b4954d6c-0451-49ed-8939-11f6568558f5`) — mine explicit feedback-ingestion tool calls. First concrete action: ground the exact captured tool-name schema against real feedback-mcp/feedback-cli/feedback-http defs (do NOT guess), then parse `event_meta.tool_arguments_json` for target `EntityUrn` + rating + note. First validation check: unit test recording exactly one `FeedbackEntry` from a synthetic tool turn, with no `content` inspection.
3. `MAP` (`16e112a7-5ab0-45a5-87c8-7d89d07ffd16`) — define failed-tool-call → entity mapping policy. First concrete action: investigate real trigger patterns in `.session/sessions/*` transcripts; decide tool-identity vs argument-referenced-entity vs unmapped. First validation check: a documented policy + typed model with an explicit "unmapped" outcome and a covering test.
4. `BFS` (`3fa60398-3154-46ee-aca5-8d87541bac1e`) — deterministic BFS entity queue. First concrete action: implement the queue over discovered entity URNs with dedupe. First validation check: a test asserting deterministic ordering + correct dedupe for a multi-entity session.
5. `SYNTH` (`3d4c4739-3138-40be-947d-556e5f7de53a`) — backtraceable/verifiable follow-up ticket format + re-enable synthesis. First concrete action: define the ticket template (links session/turn/tool-call + `FeedbackEntry` + validation ref) and re-enable synthesis in the stop-hook with a stable idempotent dedupe key. First validation check: a controlled session yields exactly one well-formed ticket and zero duplicates on re-run.

## Completion Status
- `PROV` — not-started (immediately actionable; foundational).
- `INGEST` — not-started (blocked on `PROV`).
- `MAP` — not-started (independent investigation; can run in parallel with `PROV`).
- `BFS` — not-started (blocked on `INGEST` + `MAP`).
- `SYNTH` — not-started (blocked on `INGEST` + `MAP` + `PROV`).

## Definition Of Done
Each of `PROV`, `INGEST`, `MAP`, `BFS`, `SYNTH` closed with recorded `.test` evidence; the stop-hook creates exactly one well-formed, backtraceable follow-up ticket for a controlled session and none on re-run; all feedback mining reads structured metadata only (no `SessionTurn.content` tokenization); and `ACT` (`6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc`) can then progress toward `in-review`.

The ticket index appears stale relative to disk state — `get_ticket` found ACT in `in-review` with 6 dependencies, but `next_tickets` (index-based) shows ACT as `new` with only 1 blocker, and the other 5 ticket IDs return "not found". Let me reconcile before writing the handoff.

event_meta.reasoning_text:
I'm noticing a major inconsistency here—the workspace "default" shows ACT itself in a "new" state rather than "in-review", with only one blocker in "in-implementation", while the other five dependency IDs are completely missing from the store. This contradicts what the summary indicated about all six items.

It seems like there might be two separate ticket stores at play: the root context-engine/.ticket store (which is "default"/active) and a nested memory-api/.ticket store. My first get_ticket call for ACT returned it as "in-review" with all six dependencies listed, but then the individual lookups for those five IDs failed as "entity not found" in the "default" workspace, and next_tickets shows a completely different state—this is contradictory within the same session.

The issue is likely a caching/index staleness problem. The get_ticket tool reads directly from disk (ticket.toml) at the resolved path, while next_tickets relies on a cached SQLite index that hasn't been reconciled. The get_ticket resolved ACT by searching across nested stores and found it in memory-api's store, but next_tickets is using an aggregated index that's out of sync—it's only picking up an older version of ACT before it was fully populated, and treating the other five as not-yet-indexed.

The ticket-system instructions mention reconciling the index via `ticket scan --force` and warn to confirm with get/list against the default workspace before treating something as unreachable. I should run get_ticket again for 6b0002bf to reconfirm, then try get_ticket for the other five using the memory-api workspace path explicitly to see if the stale index is the real issue.

# Summary

Define a first-class feedback platform contract where `feedback-api` owns persistent, versioned feedback records and the feedback ring is a distributed architectural property across owning domains, not a single module.

## Motivation ("why")

The current feedback subsystem was embedded inside `rule-api`, leaving no explicit crate boundary for ingestion/query/governance and enabling done-state tickets to certify a domain that had no owning crate. This contract establishes `feedback-api` as the canonical store and schema owner so dependents can rely on durable feedback behavior.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on a first-class `feedback-api` crate exposing a single versioned `FeedbackEntry` contract with persisted ingestion, inbox/query access, summary aggregation, and distributed ring persistence semantics where each edge is owned by its domain (`spec-api` execution->verified recompute, `session-api` transcript mining, `ticket-api` missing-rule follow-up, `feedback-api` frontend/user ingest). Dependents mining structured session signals can rely on: (a) `FeedbackProvenance` carrying session-turn/tool-call backtrace refs (schema v2), (b) explicit-ingestion and failed-tool-call signals being mined only from `CopilotHookEvent`s (never assumed from transcript turns, which have no `role: tool` entries in real data), (c) a grounded, never-guessing failed-tool-call-to-entity mapping policy, (d) a deterministic deduped BFS entity-discovery queue, and (e) idempotent, backtraceable follow-up ticket synthesis gated to successful non-helpful ingestion signals.

## Guards

The verification of this specification contract is gated by:
- `val-feedback-api-store-roundtrip` (ensures ingest/write/read round-trips for `FeedbackEntry`, usage, and rating events)
- `val-feedback-api-ring-persistence` (ensures distributed domain-owned ring edges persist feedback artifacts)
- `val-feedback-api-transport-surface` (ensures CLI, MCP, and HTTP entrypoints expose ingest, inbox/query, mine, and summary paths)
- `vt-feedback-provenance-backtrace` (`FeedbackProvenance` session/turn/tool-call backtrace refs round-trip and pre-v2 compatibility)
- `vt-feedback-explicit-ingestion-mining` (explicit feedback-ingestion tool calls mined from structured session events)
- `vt-feedback-failed-tool-call-mapping` (failed-tool-call to entity mapping policy; grounded, never-guessing)
- `vt-feedback-bfs-entity-queue` (deterministic deduped BFS entity-discovery queue)
- `vt-feedback-followup-synthesis` (backtraceable, idempotent follow-up ticket synthesis)

## Positions

- Core schema + store ownership: `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/lib.rs](./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/lib.rs) — schema v2 adds `turn_sequence`/`tool_call_id` backtrace refs and `FeedbackProvenance::from_session_turn`.
- Persistent NDJSON store operations: `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/feedback_store.rs](./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/feedback_store.rs)
- Rule API dependency inversion (`rule-api -> feedback-api`): `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/feedback.rs](./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/feedback.rs)
- Frontend/user ingestion edge: `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/frontend.rs](./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/frontend.rs)
- Execution->verified recompute edge: `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/verification.rs](./memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/verification.rs)
- Transcript mining edge: `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs) — event-based `mine_explicit_ingestion_signals`, `mine_failed_tool_call_signals`, grounded `map_failed_tool_call_to_entity`/`FailedToolCallMapping`/`UnmappedReason`, and `EntityDiscoveryQueue` BFS mining.
- Follow-up ticket synthesis edge: `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/follow_up.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/follow_up.rs) — re-enabled behind a backtraceable description format and idempotent UUIDv5 ticket keying; wired into the Stop hook at [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs).
- Missing-rule follow-up orchestration edge: `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/missing_rule.rs](./memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/missing_rule.rs)
- Rule no-match signal seam: `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/no_match.rs](./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/no_match.rs)
- CLI transport: `implemented` at [./memory-api/tools/cli/feedback-cli/src/main.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/feedback-cli/src/main.rs)
- MCP transport: `implemented` at [./memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp/src/server.rs](./memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp/src/server.rs)
- HTTP transport: `implemented` at [./memory-api/tools/http/feedback-http/src/lib.rs](./memory-api/tools/http/feedback-http/src/lib.rs)
- Ring activation wiring (discovery/collection/analyzer loop end-to-end): `partial` — code-level mining/synthesis edges above are implemented and unit/e2e tested; live end-to-end firing against a real production session is not yet verified (tracked by ACT below).

## Governing-rule requirement

This specification is governed and introduced by:
- [shared/instructions/spec-system/spec-system-guidance/spec-authoring-workflow/structure-the-spec/l52](shared/instructions/spec-system/spec-system-guidance/spec-authoring-workflow/structure-the-spec/l52)
